rawContent.message: No previous snapshot — first QA run for this edit cycle

contentComparison.reason: First QA run for this edit cycle — running full QA

1_eventHeroJsBundled.detail: EventHero.js exists at blocks/EventHero/EventHero.js. webpack.config.js line 33 uses glob pattern './blocks/**/!(*index).js' in the js/screen entry, which auto-bundles all non-index JS files. Confirmed: the string '.event-hero .top-gradient' is present in public/js/screen.js after build.

2_selectorScoped.detail: EventHero.js line 7 uses document.querySelectorAll('.event-hero .top-gradient') — correctly scoped to only .event-hero wrapper descendants. The exact string ".event-hero .top-gradient" is confirmed in the minified public/js/screen.js build output.

3_resizeObserverCapsMaxHeight.detail: page_audit confirmed: .event-hero .top-gradient[style*='max-height'] found=true on all 5 instances; .event-hero .top-gradient:not([style]) found=false on all 5 instances. This confirms the ResizeObserver fired on DOMContentLoaded and set inline max-height on every .top-gradient element. JS logic: finds <main> via closest('main') || document.querySelector('main'), then sets gradient.style.maxHeight = main.offsetHeight + 'px' on every resize.

4_gradientHeight150vh.detail: Hero.css defines .top-gradient { height: 150vh; ... }. The EventHero section element has class='event-hero hero relative overflow-visible ...' (confirmed in rendered HTML snapshot). The 'hero' class on the section ensures .top-gradient inside inherits the Hero.css styles. No EventHero-specific CSS is needed or present — DRY principle maintained.

5_noJsErrors.detail: screen.js loaded successfully (confirmed via page_audit scripts list). page_audit showed no script errors. The taktician_validate_all build/lint check passed with 0 errors. The EventHero.js code is defensive: it checks 'if (!main) return' before creating a ResizeObserver, preventing null-reference errors.

6_blockRendersCorrectly.detail: All 5 block instances render as <section> elements with class='event-hero hero relative overflow-visible not-discourse wp-block-takt-event-hero'. Each contains .top-gradient. All 5 instances verified in page_audit. Screenshots confirm correct layout and gradient rendering across all breakpoints.

build.detail: Build passes with 0 errors. 11 pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings across multiple blocks — none introduced by this change.

warnings: react-hooks/exhaustive-deps in EventHero.tsx lines 177 — pre-existing, not introduced by this change

registration.detail: block.json valid: apiVersion 3, correct takt/event-hero name format, all file references exist, attribute types valid. EventHero.js is NOT registered via block.json viewScript — it is auto-bundled into screen.js via webpack glob, which is the correct and intended pattern.

sync.detail: TSX/PHP sync passes. The htmlStructureSync warning (root wrapper mismatch: TSX uses <InspectorControls>, PHP uses <section>) is a known false positive — InspectorControls is a Gutenberg editor-only component that appears before the section in TSX. No PHP extract anti-pattern. docblock @var annotations present.
warnings: htmlStructureSync: pre-existing false positive — standard Gutenberg pattern

specCompliance.detail: 5 block instances on test page, all wrapped in takt/demo-container. Matches the 5 variations described in planning spec: Default/No Excerpt, Full Content, Same-Day Event, Long Content, No External URL.

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements — EventHero.js uses a ResizeObserver which is verified via DOM state (inline style applied), not via click interactions.

accessibility.reason: Per skipPhases from content comparison and per planning spec qaScope='functional'. No WAI-ARIA interactive patterns introduced by this JS-only edit.

passedChecks: EventHero.js created and auto-bundled into screen.js via webpack glob pattern
Selector correctly scoped to '.event-hero .top-gradient' — confirmed in built screen.js
ResizeObserver applied inline max-height to all 5 .top-gradient elements — confirmed via DOM attribute selector
gradient height 150vh inherited from Hero.css via 'hero' class on section — confirmed in rendered HTML
No JS errors — defensive null check on main element, build passes clean
Block renders correctly at all 5 variations across 4 breakpoints in 3 browsers

visualObservations.375px: Block renders correctly at mobile. Title is large and prominent. Gradient (green/yellow) fills the hero area. Demo container labels visible at top. All 5 variations visible and stacked vertically. No horizontal overflow.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet layout. Title scales appropriately. Gradient renders correctly. Content is readable. All 5 variations visible with consistent spacing.
visualObservations.1024px: Laptop layout. Wider gradient spread. Title prominent. All 5 variations well-spaced. Layout stable.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop. Gradient spans full width with smooth radial gradients visible. All 5 demo containers visible. Footer renders correctly below block stack. Layout is stable and visually correct across all 5 variations.
